basics.name: Shuhan Zheng
basics.summary: Aerospace M.Eng. graduate from the University of Toronto with experience in first-principles analysis, physics-aware software, nonlinear dynamical systems, intensive computation, data-driven workflows, electronics, prototyping, and systems-level analysis.

education.institution: University of Toronto Institute for Aerospace Studies

work.name: University of Toronto Institute for Aerospace Studies
work.summary: Large data-enabled space-domain awareness research on temporarily bound objects and mission-viable asteroids.
highlights: First-author AMOS 2026 abstract accepted: Dichotomizing Temporarily Bound Objects Using Capture States.
Used statistical modeling and probabilistic neural networks to support rapid, early detection of mission-viable asteroids.

work.name: University of Toronto Near Field Cosmology Group
work.summary: Automated stellar data retrieval, sanitizing, cataloging, and preparation for machine-learning ingestion.
highlights: Built a turnkey portable data pipeline adopted by the group.

work.name: China Spallation Neutron Source, Chinese Academy of Sciences
work.summary: Developed deterministic simulation software for polarized-neutron imaging of magnetized samples.
highlights: Reduced computational time from days to hours compared with conventional Monte Carlo methods.

projects.name: AMAT - Agentic Mission Analysis Toolbox
projects.summary: Independent AI-assisted mission analysis toolbox for converting user intent into mission-ready spacecraft flight plans with interactive visualization and human-in-the-loop verification.
